statement: As of late 2025, approximately 13,000 EU legal acts (directives and regulations) had been incorporated into the EEA Agreement and transposed into Icelandic law since the Agreement entered into force in 1994. This represents the cumulative acquis adopted by Iceland through the EEA Joint Committee decision process.
source_name: EFTA Secretariat — EEA Agreement legal acts database
caveats: The exact count varies depending on whether repealed, amended, or superseded acts are included. Many acts are technical regulations with limited policy significance. The EEA-Lex database provides the authoritative count but methodology differs from EU counts of the acquis.

related_entries.statement: As of 2025, approximately 70% of EU internal market legislation has been incorporated into Icelandic law through the EEA Agreement. This covers free movement of goods, services, capital, and persons, but excludes fisheries, agriculture, and justice/home affairs.

The EEA Agreement covers the EU's four freedoms (goods, services, capital, persons) and flanking policies (competition, state aid, public procurement, company law, environment, social policy). It explicitly excludes the Common Agricultural Policy (CAP), Common Fisheries Policy (CFP), customs union, common trade policy, common foreign and security policy, and justice/home affairs.

citations.report_title: Snorri segir húsnæði verða jafndýrt þótt vextir lækki

claims.claim_text: Íslendingar innleiða nú þegar megnið af regluverki Evrópusambandsins.
